purpose: Adds a check in hci_cmd_sync_queue to return an error when the HCI_UNREGISTER flag is set, preventing commands from being queued after the device is being unregistered, which could otherwise cause a use‑after‑free.
The patch deletes an unnecessary `asm/termios.h` include from n_hdlc.c, because the file already includes linux/tty.h which pulls in the proper termios header, simplifying the include hierarchy.
Updates perf c2c documentation to describe the newly added store metric value 'N/A', aligning the report table description with current behavior.
The patch fixes race conditions and lock handling for hugetlb vmas during splitting and unmapping, preventing double frees and ensuring proper lock presence for VM_MAYSHARE vmas.
Updates the ASoC dt-binding for the Realtek rt5682s codec to document AVDD and MICVDD supplies, matching their use in existing device tree files.
Adds a missing iounmap() call in zorro7xx_remove_one() to correctly release I/O memory mapped during probe, fixing a resource leak.
Deletes the unused 'work' member from struct mlx5e_async_ctx in the mlx5e driver, cleaning dead code.
Changes cfg80211_auth_request.key_idx from unsigned to signed to allow -1 sentinel value, removing static analysis warnings.
The commit reorders the entries of CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS in RISC‑V defconfig files to match the order produced by savedefconfig, without altering any functional behavior.
The patch adds a new helper function mcp251xfd_is_fd_mode() to detect CAN-FD mode and replaces scattered inline checks with calls to this function, improving code readability and maintainability.
The patch adds a guard in the RTC self‑test suite to detect the absence of an RTC device and skip the test, avoiding unnecessary failures when the hardware is not present.
The patch deletes an unused assignment to the variable 'bytes' in sound/pci/echoaudio/midi.c, eliminating a dead‑store warning from clang static analysis.
The patch modifies kernel config logic so rcuscale can be built without automatically enabling RCU Tasks in CONFIG_PREEMPT_NONE kernels, reducing kernel size and tracing complexity.
The patch edits spte.h to delete a duplicated phrase "be split" in a comment, correcting a typo without altering code logic.

The commit reverts a recent change that filled the DCN315 clock table using dcfclk values. The revert is done because the firmware leaves those entries empty when dcfclk states are disabled, leading to a no‑read/underflow condition. Restoring the previous behavior fixes the issue.
Adds a compatible string for the Renesas RZ/V2L SoC's Mali‑G31 GPU in the dt‑bindings documentation, enabling the kernel to recognize this hardware.
Renames struct pcie_port to dw_pcie_rp across the DesignWare PCIe driver to match the dw_ prefix convention, enhancing naming consistency and readability.
The patch changes the printk format string in xgbe‑drv.c to use %x instead of %hx, matching integer promotion rules and silencing clang -Wformat warnings.
The patch modifies the MIPS jump_label branch range check to cast the upper bound to a signed long, performing a signed comparison and avoiding warnings caused by negative offsets.

The patch simplifies SetHwReg8188EU() in the r8188eu driver by removing the HW_VAR_H2C_FW_P2P_PS_OFFLOAD case, which only called another function, and invokes that function directly, cleaning up the HAL layer.
Adds the static qualifier to internal symbols in arch/arm64/kernel/cpu_errata.c, limiting their visibility and silencing sparse warnings about undeclared symbols.

The commit updates the MSM display device‑tree bindings by removing an incorrect interrupt‑flags cell from MDSS nodes, because the interrupt provider supplies only a single cell. This corrects the binding specification.
The patch modifies the v4l2-ctrls code to allocate memory for array fields inside struct ctrl_ref, enabling drivers to later adjust the array size dynamically.
Adds entries for new Qualcomm SoCs (SM8350, SC8280XP, SM8450) and PMICs (SA8540P, etc.) to the socinfo driver, enabling kernel to recognize these platforms.
Expands rtl8xxxu driver chip‑cut detection to cover all cut letters A‑P, replacing the previous "unknown" output for cuts beyond E by updating the mapping logic.
The patch removes the .is_enabled flag from PRCMU clock drivers because the generic clock core already tracks enable counts, simplifying the driver code.

The patch reorganizes riscv mm init code to remove unnecessary #ifdef CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL directives by moving macros adjacent to related variables and grouping similar cases, reducing conditional compilation clutter.
The commit updates the arm64 Qualcomm SM6350 Lena device tree to enable QUP and GPI DMA hardware, preparing the platform for functional I2C nodes.
Updates comments in the rzg2l thermal driver: replaces unclear wording, fixes a typo, and adjusts comment formatting to match coding style.

The patch adds a check for failure to acquire the hardware semaphore in release_swfw_sync, logs an error and aborts, preventing the previously possible infinite loop when hardware is surprise‑removed.
The patch updates the pcm3168a ASoC driver to replace legacy bus‑clock defines with newer ASoC DAI format terminology, aligning the code with current naming conventions.
The commit deletes a conditional check for IS_SWAPFILE in the NFS code because the macro is always defined, eliminating dead code.

The commit adds handling for GTP_ECHO_REQ packets in the kernel GTP driver, sending a GTP_ECHO_RSP with appropriate information elements, to satisfy the GTP specification when no userspace daemon is present.

The patch swaps cpumask_next_* calls for cpumask_first_* when the start index is zero, adds a missing cpumask_first_zero() helper, and updates related code, improving readability and efficiency without altering behavior.

The patch deletes the driver’s call to the dtim_assoc IOVAR in brcmfmac, which was always failing because recent firmware no longer supports it, thus stopping the repeated “wl dtim_assoc failed (-52)” messages after a roam.
Adds a board configuration (adl_rt5682) to enable the alc5682 headset codec on Intel platforms that lack a speaker amplifier, connecting it to SSP0 per Intel BT offload design.
Adds a check in io_uring networking code to reject zerocopy sendmsg calls on sockets that do not support zerocopy, mirroring earlier handling for send requests.
Updates the kerneldoc-preamble script to test for xeCJK.sty before loading, preventing LaTeX errors on systems lacking the texlive-xecjk package.
The patch relaxes checkpatch.pl to accept macros that expand to case statements, preventing spurious warnings about missing parentheses.

The commit switches simpledrm from its custom simple‑KMS helper functions to the standard DRM atomic helper APIs, removes unused CRTC atomic enable/disable helpers, and always invokes drm_atomic_helper_check_plane_state(), without altering driver functionality.
Adds a JPEG decode worker interface to the MediaTek MT8195 driver, enabling three hardware JPEG decoders to run in parallel, extending driver functionality.
The commit deletes unnecessary VPRINTK() debug prints and the ata_scsi_dump_cdb() helper because tracepoints already provide that information, cleaning up libata code.
Adds AMD DRM driver support to enter zstate during extended vblank when FreeSync video mode provides extra blank lines, calculating time gain and updating registers, restoring defaults on exit.

The patch changes the hqd_destroy() callback prototype in the AMD GPU driver, making its third parameter uint32_t instead of enum kfd_preempt_type, matching existing implementations and removing a CFI type mismatch failure when compiled with CONFIG_CFI_CLANG.

Updates the STM32 pinctrl device‑tree binding: renames the property from 'reset' to the correct 'resets' and adds 'additionalProperties: false' to the schema, fixing the typo and tightening validation.
The patch suppresses a GCC 12 array‑bounds warning in ath9k's mac.c by adjusting warning levels, likely via a pragma or comment, without altering runtime behavior.
The commit updates the sun8i-ss crypto driver to replace pm_runtime_get_sync with pm_runtime_resume_and_get, simplifying runtime power management handling.
The commit moves the fixed file install/remove helpers into the filetable area and changes their API to accept a context instead of an io_kiocb, improving code organization for io_uring.

The commit updates device‑tree bindings for the NXP i.MX8MQ VPU, splitting the previously combined G1 and G2 decoder blocks into separate nodes that are independently powered by the vpu‑blk‑ctrl domain.

The commit deletes the IWL_DEVICE_BZ_COMMON macro, which was only referenced inside the IWL_DEVICE_BZ macro, and merges its contents directly, simplifying the macro definitions without altering functionality.
